purpose: Define the minimum receipt shape BuildLang/buildc should emit for scientific kernels whose outputs support proof-packet claims.

verification_layers.receipt_obligation: record workspace, head, source files, source refs, and source hash
record compiler, compiler status, target, flags, dependencies, and build hash
record runtime, operating system, hardware class, deterministic seed, and environment hash
record equation/model, domain, boundary conditions, and discretization
record metric, observed values, threshold, and units
record invariant name, expectation, observed result, tolerance, and status
record validator, Crucible, formal checker, reviewer, or lab verdicts

market_wedge.hypothesis: BuildLang/buildc can differentiate from Julia, PDE frameworks, HPC solvers, physics AI tools, and commercial simulation environments by making compiler/runtime receipts native to scientific claims.
